task.prompt: When a model generates very long repetitive streaming output (thousands of characters of the same phrase), the content loop detector sometimes fails to catch the loop. The failure is intermittent and only occurs when the output is near the internal sliding window boundary. Users see the CLI hang outputting repeated content until it hits a timeout rather than being stopped early by the loop detector. Investigate and fix the loop detection sliding window logic.

hint_levels.symptom_only: Content loop detection fails intermittently for long streaming responses near the sliding window boundary. Repetitive output is not caught and the CLI continues generating for much longer than expected.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug is in packages/core/src/services/loopDetectionService.ts in the interaction between truncateAndUpdate() and analyzeContentChunksForLoop(). When truncation occurs during streaming, lastContentIndex can become stale relative to the new history length.
hint_levels.function_hint: At lines 419-437, analyzeContentChunksForLoop() uses lastContentIndex. When truncateAndUpdate() fires mid-analysis (lines 382-407), it adjusts lastContentIndex but the while loop condition can skip the remaining window if truncation removes exactly the chunk range being processed.

reasoning_chain: Read the symptom: loop detection fails intermittently near sliding window boundary
Open loopDetectionService.ts and find the content analysis entry point
Read truncateAndUpdate() at lines 382-407 — it adjusts lastContentIndex when history exceeds MAX_HISTORY_LENGTH
Read analyzeContentChunksForLoop() at lines 419-437 — uses lastContentIndex in while loop
Trace a truncation event: history at 4950 chars, chunk arrives, truncation fires, adjusts index
Discover: if truncation removes the exact chunk range being analyzed, while loop exits prematurely
Confirm by reading turn.ts to understand event streaming timing
Read client.ts to see how streaming events feed into loop detector
Read existing test file to understand expected behavior
Apply fix: re-validate lastContentIndex bounds after each truncation event
